hero.tagline: Systematic, transparent, and publicly accessible data on
hero.taglineStrong: subnational political institutions and electoral outcomes

whySPP.title: Why SPP?
whySPP.body: Researchers studying subnational politics in Latin America have long faced the same obstacle: data on governors, legislatures, and elections was
whySPP.bodyStrong: scattered across countries, coded differently in each, and nearly impossible to compare at scale.

solution.title: A transformational resource for subnational research.
solution.body: The Subnational Politics Project addresses the long-standing challenge of data scarcity and comparability in the Global South. By offering over 40 years of systematic, transparent, and publicly accessible data, we empower deeper analysis of federalism, democracy, and territorial governance.
solution.scholarlyDesc: Analyze subnational democracy, authoritarianism, and party competition.
solution.policyDesc: Empower policymakers to identify and address territorial inequalities.
solution.teachingDesc: Provide students with unique visual tools for comparative politics.
solution.democraticDesc: Open data for journalists to hold local governments accountable.

team.title: Team
team.body: A collaborative network of Latin American scholars and researchers living abroad, bridging global academic standards with deep-rooted regional expertise.
